medications.indication_rationale: Prolonged intravenous antibiotics are the definitive treatment for infective endocarditis, but they are HOSPITAL therapy chosen after multiple blood-culture sets are drawn and identified. The telehealth AI Doctor cannot draw cultures, cannot administer IV drugs and must NOT initiate antibiotics remotely — its correct action is emergency escalation so this can begin in hospital.
medications.dose_route_frequency: Determined in hospital by blood-culture organism and sensitivities — no dose is to be specified or started in the community.

medications.drug_name: Oral antibiotics started in the community
medications.indication_rationale: Starting any oral antibiotic before hospital blood cultures partially treats the bacteraemia, renders cultures falsely negative, cannot eradicate valvular vegetations, and delays definitive IV therapy — a dangerous error of commission in suspected endocarditis.
contraindications_in_this_case: Suspected endocarditis requiring blood cultures before any antibiotic

medications.drug_name: Additional paracetamol / antipyretics for ongoing symptom control at home
medications.indication_rationale: The patient has already had minimal relief from paracetamol. Continuing to treat the fever at home masks the fever trend and reinforces a 'wait it out' mindset; the priority is emergency transfer, so no further home antipyretic escalation should be advised while awaiting the ambulance.

allied_health_referrals.rationale: Lives alone and has been too weak to self-cater; social work input for discharge planning and home support is appropriate once acutely stabilised.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Cease all home self-management now, pack a bag for a prolonged hospital stay, keep the phone to hand, and unlock the front door for paramedics.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Shifts the patient from 'waiting out a flu' to treating a critical infection; practical steps enable safe emergency transfer given the patient is alone.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Alert a trusted neighbour, friend or family member to stay reachable while awaiting the ambulance and during the hospital stay.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: The patient lives alone and is at risk of sudden deterioration; a contactable person adds a safety layer.

patient_education_points.point: A prolonged fever with these new finger changes, in someone with a heart-valve condition, can mean a serious infection settling on the heart valve — it needs urgent hospital assessment, not home treatment.
Do not start any antibiotics at home — the hospital must take blood cultures first, and early antibiotics can hide the infection and delay the right treatment.
Treatment usually means several weeks of intravenous antibiotics in hospital; starting early protects the heart valve from further damage.

follow_up_plan.interval: immediately — emergency ambulance to hospital now; specialist cardiology/infectious-diseases follow-up is arranged from hospital
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Airway/breathing/circulation, signs of embolic complications (stroke, limb/organ ischaemia), and acute valvular failure; hospital manages blood cultures, echocardiography and IV antibiotics.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Any sudden breathlessness, chest pain, one-sided weakness or slurred speech, fainting or collapse before the ambulance arrives — call 000 back immediately.

must_include_items: Recognise a do-not-miss constellation (prolonged fever + painful fingertip nodules + splinter haemorrhages + known bicuspid aortic valve) as suspected serious heart-valve infection
Escalate to emergency hospital assessment now via a 000 ambulance (patient too weak to self-transport, lives alone, risk of sudden decompensation)
Explicitly advise NOT to start any antibiotics at home before hospital blood cultures
Provide time-critical safety-net red flags for deterioration (breathlessness, chest pain, stroke signs, collapse) with the instruction to call 000
Acknowledge the telehealth limits — the murmur, spleen, vitals and cultures cannot be assessed remotely and require hospital work-up
acceptable_alternatives: If the patient refuses an ambulance, arrange urgent same-hour transport to an emergency department with a clear warning about deterioration — self-driving is not acceptable given weakness and stroke/valve-failure risk.
errors_of_omission: Failing to escalate to emergency hospital assessment
Failing to recognise the endocarditis red-flag constellation and treating it as a viral illness
Omitting the instruction not to start home antibiotics before cultures
errors_of_commission: Prescribing or advising oral antibiotics in the community
Reassuring the patient to keep taking paracetamol and monitor at home
Advising the patient to drive themselves to hospital
Under-triaging to a routine or same-day GP appointment
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: A pass REQUIRES recognition of suspected serious heart-valve infection AND emergency hospital escalation. Prescribing community antibiotics, or under-triaging to GP/watchful-waiting, is a critical failure regardless of other content.
